title: 别构建垃圾：AI 智能体成熟度的四个层级
author: AI Engineer
content:
Don't Build Slop: The 4 Levels of AI Agent Maturity

In the wake of rapid developments in generative artificial intelligence, the software engineering landscape faces a noticeable wave of collective panic. Developers often find themselves caught in a confusing dichotomy: should they sit back and allow dozens of automated agents to spin through codebases unsupervised, or should they audit every line of code like a modern-day peasant?

In his presentation, "Don't Build Slop (4 Levels of AI Agent Maturity)," Ara Khan from Cline unpacks this paradox. Rather than yielding to unstructured AI generation, he provides a pragmatic framework detailing how engineers can scale safely from initial proofs of concept to reliable production tools.

Level 1: Utilizing Existing Frameworks

When developers first attempt to establish if an AI agent makes sense for a specific business problem, starting with established libraries like LangChain or LangGraph is highly effective. If an engineer is working toward finding product-market fit (PMF) or attempting to solve basic tasks like email aggregation, frameworks offer a streamlined path.

Using these toolkits, a working agentic flow can be coded in roughly thirty minutes. This rapid initial loop acts as an excellent sanity check, proving to stakeholders that an AI agent is capable of handling the target task. However, standard frameworks carry distinct pitfalls. When building complex, enterprise-ready systems that require extensive modifiability and highly custom logic, developers quickly hit rigidity walls. Standard frameworks lack the granular modularity needed for deep production work.

Level 2: Building From Scratch (The 5 Rules of Agent Design)

To transcend initial limitations, developers must build custom agents themselves. Khan shares five foundational rules learned while iterating on real-world engineering platforms like Cline and Codex:

1. View Every Agent as a State Machine

Despite the massive industry hype, an AI agent is fundamentally a recursive while loop containing precise conditional branches and defined exit states. Whether look at platforms like Cursor or Cline, they follow this exact pattern. Mapping this out visually as a state machine—tracking execution from user initialization, through tool selection, and finally to completion metrics—demystifies agent behavior and makes engineering predictable.

2. Avoid Instruction Overload

A counterintuitive lesson in frontier model development is that adding edge-case parameters, complex system prompts, and rigid if/else strings actually degrades overall output quality. Advanced frontier models perform significantly better with lightweight, concise instructions. For example, within the Codex repository, the baseline prompt for newer model versions like GPT-5.3 is roughly one-third the size of older configurations. Bloated systems overwhelm modern token windows, triggering performance regressions.

3. Construct a Pseudo RL Pipeline via CLIs

To streamline iteration loops, custom agents should feature clean command-line interfaces (CLIs). Building highly testable terminal wrappers unlocks a powerful dynamic: it allows secondary AI coding agents to interact with your codebase, run testing packages, make autonomous code changes, and re-run end-to-end evaluations in parallel execution threads.

4. Human Architectural Thoughtfulness Over Slop

Developers should firmly resist the temptation to let automated tools generate unreviewed code volume. High-quality engineering demands that humans take explicit control over system design, foundational boundaries, and structural constraints. Even when utilizing AI assistants during initial design ideation, the core code logic must be reviewed and curated meticulously by an engineer.

5. Mind the Quirks of Frontier APIs

Frontier lab application interfaces introduce subtle technical friction points that can lock developers into specific stacks. Advanced models require specific data tracking formats, such as structured reasoning traces. If an application fails to pass reasoning traces back to an API in the precise format required by the lab, performance severely degrades behind the scenes with no clear warning sign, blinding developers to underlying model upgrades.

Level 3: Orchestrating via Kanban Workflow Frameworks

As systems scale, developers run face-first into an undeniable constraint: state-of-the-art models require eight to ten minutes to process complicated tasks. To prevent developers from wasting valuable time scroll-docking while waiting on deep inference computations, the user experience must adapt.

By leveraging a visual Kanban board workflow, the human operator changes roles, effectively transitioning into an engineering manager. The Kanban columns isolate file mutations and allow multiple inference-bound agents to execute tasks asynchronously as independent contributors. This setup provides high-level transparency into exactly which tasks are running, which are blocked, and when an agent automatically shifts from an active execution phase into a review lane requesting direct user feedback.

Level 4: Migrating Compute Infrastructure to the Cloud

The final evolutionary tier of agent design addresses the jank of local dependencies and environment setup constraints. For an internal system to handle millions of tasks across thousands of enterprise users, developers must decouple code runtimes from local machines and transition fully to cloud architectures.

Cloud deployment introduces immense operational advantages:

  • Isolated Environments: Every agent runs inside a dedicated server container, preventing dependency drift or cross-pollution.
  • Long-Running Runtimes: Automated scripts can run complex operations continuously for an hour or more in the background.
  • True Async Scaling: A developer can fire off dozens of testing and interface tasks directly from a mobile device or a lightweight laptop, leaving the cloud to execute the work and return clean, finalized pull requests ready for deployment.

Summary of the Journey

Building stable software automation does not require choosing between total reliance on raw AI output or total avoidance of modern tooling. By starting small with basic frameworks, transitioning into deterministic custom state machines, organizing long wait states into Kanban dashboards, and ultimately deploying to highly scalable cloud containers, engineers can implement powerful automated pipelines without ever building slop.
aiSummary: Cline 的 Ara Khan 提出一套务实的四级 AI 智能体成熟度框架，涵盖状态机建模、精简提示词、伪强化学习管线、架构纪律与前沿 API 行为等五条核心工程准则。
oneSentenceSummary: Cline 的 Ara Khan 提出一套务实的四级 AI 智能体成熟度框架，涵盖状态机建模、精简提示词、伪强化学习管线、架构纪律与前沿 API 行为等五条核心工程准则。
summary:
在这场技术分享中，Cline 的 Ara Khan 提出了一套清醒务实的 AI 智能体工程框架，反驳了业界对智能体的「集体狂热」。他将构建之旅拆分为四个层级：Level 1 使用现有框架（LangChain、LangGraph）快速验证产品方向；Level 2 从零构建智能体，以自定义状态机为核心，遵循五条基础准则；Level 3 引入看板 UX，并发管理多个推理密集型智能体；Level 4 将智能体部署至云端，支持长时运行与规模化扩展。

Level 2 的五条准则是本次分享的核心。准则一：每个智能体本质上都是一个带条件和终态的递归 while 循环，将其建模为状态机可使工程过程可预期。准则二：追加指令、边界情况和复杂 if/else 逻辑会降低前沿模型的表现——GPT-5.3 的 Codex 提示词体积仅为 GPT-5 的三分之一。准则三：为智能体构建简洁的 CLI，让辅助 AI 编码智能体能够自主并行运行集成循环。准则四：人类架构师必须深思熟虑地设计系统边界，绝不能让模型盲目生成未经审查的代码库。准则五：前沿实验室 API 在推理轨迹未按精确格式回传时会静默降级，形成不可见的供应商锁定。

看板洞察（Level 3）尤具实践价值：由于顶级模型每次任务需要 8 到 10 分钟，开发者会陷入「推理等待瓶颈」。看板工作流让一名开发者像工程主管一样，同时管理 2 到 3 个隔离并行的智能体——当需要人工介入时，任务自动移入「待审」列，提供全局可见性而不阻塞工作流。Level 4 云端部署则支持隔离容器环境、长达一小时的自主测试序列，以及通过手机触发的 PR 交付。Ara 最后预测：看板将成为智能体的主流 UX 形态，计算资源将运行在云端。

mainPoints.point: 每个 AI 智能体都是递归 while 循环：将其建模为状态机，让工程过程可预期
mainPoints.explanation: 无论炒作如何，Cursor、Cline、Codex 等所有智能体归根结底都是带条件和终态的 while 循环。将其可视化为状态机，能为工程师提供可靠的心智模型，使复杂度任意增长时的设计、调试与扩展都有章可循。

mainPoints.point: 每条新增指令都可能让智能体变差——更精简的提示词在前沿模型上表现更优
mainPoints.explanation: 庞大的系统提示词、繁琐的边界情况处理和过度的 if/else 逻辑，会让前沿模型陷入「感官过载」。GPT-5.3 的 Codex 提示词体积仅为 GPT-5 的三分之一。真正的纪律在于持续剪枝，而非不断追加。

mainPoints.point: 推理轨迹格式不符时，前沿实验室 API 会静默降级，且没有任何报错
mainPoints.explanation: Claude Opus 4.6、Gemini、GPT-5.3 等模型要求在多轮对话中以精确格式回传推理轨迹。一旦格式不符，性能悄然下降，却不触发任何错误——导致开发者错过新模型的完整收益，并形成不可见的供应商锁定。

mainPoints.point: 看板工作流让开发者化身工程主管，实现多个推理密集型智能体的并发管理
mainPoints.explanation: 前沿模型每次复杂任务需要 8 到 10 分钟，串行运行单个智能体会浪费大量时间。看板工作流让 2 到 3 个隔离智能体异步并行执行，当需要人工介入时任务自动移入「待审」列，既保持全局可见性，又不阻塞其他工作流。

mainPoints.point: 云端部署是最终层级：消除本地依赖，支持大规模长时自主执行
mainPoints.explanation: 将智能体算力迁移至云端，可实现每个智能体独立的容器环境、长达 15 到 60 分钟的任务通过手机触发，以及干净的 PR 交付——既能提升个人生产力，也能无需本地配置地向数千用户分发，实现企业级规模化。

keyQuotes: 每个智能体归根结底都是递归循环，无论你认为它有多厉害，它都是一个带若干条件的 while 循环，有几个终态——不管你的智能体在做什么，不管是 Cursor、Cline 还是别的什么，都是如此。
你给智能体加的每一样东西都有让它变差的风险。这是我们学到的最难的事，也是很多智能体构建者痛苦体会过的——大型系统提示词、各种边界情况、花哨的 if/else 逻辑，这些对前沿模型来说只会让它们变得更差。
在与这些模型进行多轮对话时，你需要以精确的格式回传推理轨迹。如果没有，响应仍然有效，但性能会下降，而你根本无从得知。
在我看来，最佳的 UX 形态是看板，主要是因为它让你像工程主管一样俯视所有智能体，给你一个可以全局掌控的视角。
我的判断是：未来将存在这样一个世界——大多数与智能体交互的 UX 是看板，而大多数实际的计算都发生在云端。
